headline: Apple's foldable iPhone faces engineering snags, potential shipment delays, Nikkei Asia reports
summary: Apple has been encountering setbacks in the engineering test ‌phase of its first foldable iPhone, ‌which could lead to delays in its mass ​production and product shipment schedule, Nikkei Asia reported on Monday, citing sources.  Here are some details:  * Engineering development issues could delay the ‌firstshipments of ⁠the foldable iPhones by months in a worst-casescenario, according to the ⁠Nikkei report.  * "It's true that more issues than expected have emergedduring the early test ​production phase, ​and additional time ​willbe needed to resolve ‌them and make necessary adjustments," thereport said, quoting one source familiar with the matter.

content.title: Apple's foldable iPhone faces engineering snags, potential shipment delays, Nikkei Asia reports
content.summary: Apple has been encountering setbacks in the engineering test ‌phase of its first foldable iPhone, ‌which could lead to delays in its mass ​production and product shipment schedule, Nikkei Asia reported on Monday, citing sources. headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Apple, UnitedHealth, Broadcom, Arm, Delta, Intel, Arista, and More
summary: Stocks were sliding Tuesday after President Donald Trump declared that “a whole civilization will die tonight” ahead of his 8 p.m. ET deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  Apple  tumbled 3.4% after Nikkei Asia reported that the iPhone maker’s long-awaited foldable phone had encountered engineering challenges and could face shipping delays.  The federal government on Monday pledged to pay insurers in the Medicare program an average increase of 2.48% next year, well above an originally proposed rate of just 0.09% in January.

content.title: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Apple, UnitedHealth, Broadcom, Arm, Delta, Intel, Arista, and More
content.summary: Stocks were sliding Tuesday after President Donald Trump declared that “a whole civilization will die tonight” ahead of his 8 p.m. ET deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  Apple  tumbled 3.4% after Nikkei Asia reported that the iPhone maker’s long-awaited foldable phone had encountered engineering challenges and could face shipping delays.  The federal government on Monday pledged to pay insurers in the Medicare program an average increase of 2.48% next year, well above an originally proposed rate of just 0.09% in January.

headline: Apple Stock Drops on Foldable iPhone Delay Fears
summary: Apple  stock was falling Tuesday after a report said the tech giant could be facing delays in launching its highly anticipated foldable iPhone.  The  Apple  release has been expected for months, but customers and investors might have to wait a little longer, according to a report by Nikkei Asia.  Apple is dealing with issues in the engineering test phase of the foldable iPhone, which could lead to delays in its production and shipment schedule, Nikkei Asia reported, citing sources familiar.

headline: Anthropic touts AI cybersecurity project with Big Tech partners
summary: Anthropic on Tuesday announced an initiative with major technology companies, including Amazon.com, Microsoft and Apple, that lets partners preview ‌an advanced model with cybersecurity capabilities developed by the AI startup.  Under ‌its "Project Glasswing", select organizations will be allowed to use the startup's unreleased and general-purpose AI model, "Claude ​Mythos Preview", for defensive cybersecurity work, Anthropic said.  Other partners include CrowdStrike, Palo Alto Networks, Google and Nvidia.

headline: Apple’s Foldable iPhone Remains on Track for September Debut
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Apple Inc.’s first foldable phone is on track to arrive during the company’s normal iPhone launch period later this year, people with knowledge of the matter said, rebutting concerns about major manufacturing snags. headline: Apple's global sales for iPhone 17 surged in February

headline: Stock Market Today: Dow, S&P 500 Close Mixed As Critical Iran Deadline Nears (Live Coverage)
summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average and other major stock indexes closed the session modestly mixed Tuesday with President Donald Trump's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz quickly approaching.  In stock news, UnitedHealth Group bucked the trend with a hefty gain and was the biggest Dow winner on the stock market today.  Stocks jockeyed around in the final hour of trading with Dow ending 0.2% lower while the S&P 500 reversed and ended 0.1% higher.

headline: Apple Weighs AI Security Push And China Risks As Shares Trade Below Targets
summary: Apple joined Amazon, Microsoft, and Anthropic in Project Glasswing, a new AI powered cybersecurity initiative, signaling deeper collaboration on digital protection for users and enterprises. Engineering issues reported around Apple's first foldable iPhone are raising the prospect of launch delays, potentially pushing back its entry into the foldable handset segment. In China, the Supreme People's Court rejected Apple's appeal to invalidate Xiao I Corporation's AI patents, confirming Xiao I's...
